query: Historical frequency of pre-season Atlantic named storms before June 1 by year, how many years out of last 30 had a named storm before June 1
answer:
Based on my research (primarily from training data, as live web tools were unavailable), here is my comprehensive synthesis:

---

## Pre-Season Atlantic Named Storms Before June 1: Historical Frequency (1996–2025)

### Overview

The official Atlantic hurricane season runs from June 1 to November 30. However, named storms occasionally form before June 1 — termed "pre-season" storms. This research covers the 30-year window from 1996 through 2025.

### Complete List of Pre-Season Named Storms (Before June 1), 1996–2025

| Year | Storm Name | Formation Date | Notes |
|------|------------|----------------|-------|
| **2003** | Tropical Storm Ana | April 20 | Formed as subtropical, transitioned to tropical April 21 |
| **2007** | Subtropical Storm Andrea | May 9 | Remained subtropical throughout lifecycle |
| **2012** | Tropical Storm Alberto | May 19 | Formed off the Carolinas |
| **2012** | Tropical Storm Beryl | May 26 | Made landfall in northeastern Florida |
| **2015** | Tropical Storm Ana | May 8 | Formed off the Carolinas, made landfall in SC |
| **2016** | Hurricane Alex | January 14 | Extremely rare January hurricane; formed near the Azores |
| **2016** | Tropical Storm Bonnie | May 27 | Formed near the Carolinas |
| **2020** | Tropical Storm Arthur | May 16 | Part of the record-breaking 2020 season |
| **2020** | Tropical Storm Bertha | May 27 | Made landfall in South Carolina |
| **2021** | Subtropical Storm Ana | May 22 | Formed near Bermuda |

### Years with NO Pre-Season Named Storms (1996–2025)

1996, 1997, 1998, 1999, 2000, 2001, 2002, 2004, 2005, 2006, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2013, 2014, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2022, 2023, 2024

*(Note: 2024's first named storm, Alberto, formed around June 19; 2022's first, Alex, formed June 5; 2025 and 2026 data is uncertain from my sources.)*

### Summary Statistics

- **Years with at least one pre-season named storm: 7 out of 30** (1996–2025)
- **Frequency: ~23.3%** of years
- **Total pre-season named storms in 30-year period: 10**
- **Years with multiple pre-season storms: 3** (2012, 2016, 2020 — each with 2)

### Trend Analysis

- **1996–2010 (15 years):** Only **2 years** had pre-season storms (2003, 2007) — **13.3%** frequency
- **2011–2025 (15 years):** At least **5 years** had pre-season storms (2012, 2015, 2016, 2020, 2021) — **33.3%** frequency
- This suggests a **notable increase** in pre-season storm frequency in the more recent 15-year period, roughly doubling.

### Geographic and Seasonal Patterns

- **Most pre-season storms form in May**, particularly mid-to-late May.
- Common formation areas: off the southeast U.S. coast (Carolinas, Florida) and near Bermuda, where warm Gulf Stream waters can support tropical development.
- **Hurricane Alex (January 2016)** is an extreme outlier — a January hurricane is extraordinarily rare.

### Policy Context

- NOAA began issuing tropical weather outlooks starting **May 15** (rather than June 1) beginning in 2024, acknowledging the increasing trend of pre-season activity.
- The official season dates (June 1–November 30) remain unchanged, but the earlier outlook window reflects operational recognition of this trend.

### Data Limitations

- This analysis is based on training data through early 2025. Live web sources were unavailable for verification.
- The 2025 and 2026 seasons could not be fully verified. Any pre-season storms in those years are not captured here.
- Exact formation dates may vary by ±1 day depending on best-track vs. operational classifications.

---

key_facts: 7 out of 30 years (1996–2025) had at least one Atlantic named storm form before June 1, a frequency of approximately 23.3%.
10 total pre-season named storms formed before June 1 in the 30-year period 1996–2025.
Three years had two pre-season storms each: 2012 (Alberto, Beryl), 2016 (Alex, Bonnie), and 2020 (Arthur, Bertha).
Pre-season storms have become more frequent: 13.3% of years (2/15) in 1996–2010 vs. 33.3% (5/15) in 2011–2025.
Most pre-season named storms form in May, typically off the southeast U.S. coast (Carolinas, Florida) or near Bermuda.
Hurricane Alex (January 14, 2016) is the most extreme outlier — a rare January Atlantic hurricane forming near the Azores.
NOAA began issuing tropical weather outlooks starting May 15 (rather than June 1) beginning in 2024, acknowledging increasing pre-season activity.
The years with pre-season named storms were: 2003, 2007, 2012, 2015, 2016, 2020, and 2021.
The earliest pre-season storm in the 30-year period was Hurricane Alex on January 14, 2016; the earliest May storm was Tropical Storm Ana on May 8, 2015.
